title: The Atlantic Monthly, Volume 14, No. 81, July, 1864: A Magazine of Literature, Art, and Politics
summaries: “The Atlantic Monthly, Volume 14, No. 81, July, 1864” by Various is a literary magazine published in the mid-19th century. The story unfolds around a pivotal moment in her life when her husband, Doctor Manning, reveals his financial troubles, prompting Hester to grapple with feelings of despair and the sense of lost ambition. Through her memories and observations, she conveys a struggle between her familial duties and her longing for personal fulfillment, illustrating the tension between personal aspirations and societal roles for women during that era.
